role_hints.role: Commissioned notaries / signing agents (owners and staff)
role_hints.oews_basis: role_mix is 4-digit 5411 (lawyer-dominated: 23-1011 Lawyers 62.15%, 23-2011 Paralegals 13.12%) — that mix describes law offices, not notary offices, and should be treated as unusable for this code; structure below is hypothesis from industry knowledge
role_hints.note: The statutory witnessing act itself — physical or live audio-video presence of a commissioned human — is the product and cannot be done by AI under current law in every state. Everything around it (document prep, journaling, scheduling, ID-check workflow) automates; RON platforms already automate most of it.

role_hints.oews_basis: hypothesis — 43-xxxx office/admin occupations in the 5411 tail; role_mix effectively empty for this code
role_hints.note: Order intake, appointment routing, confirmations and status tracking are fully automatable — signing platforms (Snapdocs-style) already do this at scale.

role_hints.oews_basis: hypothesis — not represented in the 5411 mix
role_hints.note: Physical transport of wet-ink documents persists only where e-recording/e-closing has not displaced it; shrinking independently of AI.

source_hints.source: National Notary Association (NNA) publications and RON state-law tracking
source_hints.why: The trade body; tracks remote online notarization enactment (now the large majority of states) and notary population — adoption of RON is the relevant technology-adoption axis here.

source_hints.source: ALTA Digital Closing Survey (American Land Title Association, annual)
source_hints.why: e-closing and RON penetration in mortgage signings — the main paid use case for signing agents.

source_hints.source: State statutory notary fee schedules (per-act fee caps set in statute, compiled by NNA)
source_hints.why: Primary evidence that per-act price is legally capped in many states — the pricing-capture mechanism is statutory, not market.

source_hints.source: Proof (formerly Notarize) and Snapdocs company disclosures / published volume claims
source_hints.why: Vendor-side evidence on whether the platform layer, not the notary office, captures RON economics.

source_hints.source: MBA (Mortgage Bankers Association) origination volume forecasts
source_hints.why: Demand is derivative of real-estate/mortgage transaction volume — needed to normalize any single-year revenue evidence.
Expect a near-empty comp set for notary offices; the absence of an M&A market is itself the finding for M.

capture_questions: Per-act notarial fees are capped by statute in many states (e.g. a fixed dollar maximum per acknowledgment) and loan-signing fees are set by title/escrow buyers: when AI/RON automation cuts the cost per signing, do capped fees and platform-set payouts mean the saving accrues to platforms and title companies rather than the notary office? Name the fee mechanism for the dominant revenue stream (per-act statutory fee vs. negotiated signing-agent fee).

Do title companies and lenders route signings through platforms (Snapdocs, Proof, DocuSign Notary), making the individual notary interchangeable gig labor with no client ownership — who actually owns the repeat relationship?
What stops the end customer from using a RON platform directly, bypassing any 'office of notaries' entirely — is there any firm-level moat beyond a local reputation and a commission certificate?
terminal_value_question: This service exists purely by statute: a commissioned human must witness. The terminal test is two-layered — (1) if legislatures ever accept cryptographic identity verification without a human witness, paid notarization disappears outright; (2) even if the human requirement persists, does the RON/signing-platform layer capture the remaining economics, leaving notary offices as piece-rate labor at statutorily capped fees? Distinguish AI cutting an office's back-office costs from AI-plus-platforms replacing the office as the seller.
special_notes: Data quality warning: the record's n_total is 0 with source 'no SUSB or QCEW series found' and the role_mix is the 4-digit 5411 lawyer mix — essentially no code-specific government data exists. Treat every industry-level figure for this code as an estimate and say so in the output.

Pricing-model deflation candidate: statutory per-act fee caps are a hard price ceiling — no operator pricing power on the notarial act itself; margin can only come from volume, ancillary services and signing-agent fees, which platforms are compressing.

Most establishments are solo/mobile operators with near-zero transferable enterprise value — distinguish 'the industry exists' from 'acquirable firms with $1-10M EBITDA exist'; the honest answer to the latter may be near-none.
Demand is derivative of mortgage and real-estate transaction volume — highly cyclical; normalize against MBA origination data.

Boundary confusion: 541191 title abstract and settlement offices embed notarization as one step — do not let title/settlement data contaminate this code; also distinguish from the general-purpose UPS-store-style notary counter (retail, not 541120 firms).

research_gaps.instruction: The dataset record could not compute n_band (derivation says: 'DATA GAP: no SUSB size-class data for this code; n_band could not be computed — the research run must supply it with a shown chain, quality ESTIMATE'), and n_total is also 0 ('no SUSB or QCEW series found'). As the documented exception to rule 6, research this yourself: estimate the number of notary-office firms from the best available evidence (state notary commission counts via NNA as the population ceiling, signing-service directories, or the 5411 parent's size distribution as a shape prior), then apply the record's partial data — revenue/employee $317,971 at 5411 (almost certainly far too high for notary offices; source a notary-specific revenue-per-operator figure instead) and a sourced margin — to derive how many firms could clear $1M EBITDA. Show the full chain firm-count → revenue/firm → EBITDA/firm → band membership. The plausible honest answer is that n_band is approximately zero; if so, state that with the chain shown. Mark the result quality ESTIMATE and list it in top_uncertain_inputs with its range.
